name: Nadia Boulanger
abstract: Juliette Nadia Boulanger (French: [ʒyljɛt nadja bulɑ̃ʒe] ; 16 September 1887 – 22 October 1979) was a French music teacher, conductor and composer. She taught many of the leading composers and musicians of the twentieth century, and also performed occasionally as a pianist and organist.

works.t: Concerto for Two Pianos in E-flat Major, K. 365

works_created.t: Les heures claires: 3. Vous m'avez dit
Les heures claires: 5. C'était en juin
Morceau de concours pour le Conservatoire
Les heures claires: 8. S'il arrive jamais

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Well it’s a composition and that sort of thing from Nadia Boulanger. And was able to use a lot of what he learned in his own music
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Dr. Bodley, who taught harmony and some composition, who had studied with Nadia Boulanger
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: It’s a combination of a lot of things, from Basie to Ray Charles to Clark Terry to Nadia Boulanger. They don’t get any better than her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Donald Byrd, Herbie Hancock, and Quincy Jones had spent time in Paris before that, studying with that great theory teacher over there, Nadia Boulanger.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
